meta.title: E-PRid — EPR Certificate Verification for Battery, Tyre & Used Oil
meta.description: Risk scores on every recycler and certificate before you buy, plus a guided registration assistant for used-oil collection agents. Covering battery, tyre, and used-oil EPR in India.

hero.titleLine1: Know if your EPR certificate is real
hero.body: India's plastic EPR system saw ₹700Cr+ in fraudulent certificates before anyone caught it. Battery and tyre EPR run the same certificate architecture. E-PRid gives producers, PROs, and compliance consultancies a risk score on every recycler and certificate before they buy — plus a guided registration assistant for used-oil collection agents navigating CPCB's sign-up process.
hero.footnote: Covering Battery, Tyre, and Used-Oil EPR under India's Hazardous & Other Waste Rules

recyclers.desc: Registered battery recyclers — with no independent verification layer

fraud.desc: Fraudulent plastic EPR certs undetected. Battery and tyre EPR use the same architecture.

deadline.desc: Annual return deadline — with no way to verify what you're filing

streams.heading: Three waste streams, one verification layer
streams.subheading: Battery and tyre get an independent risk-check. Used-oil gets something different: help getting into the system in the first place.

battery.title: Battery EPR
battery.body: Risk-check any recycler or certificate — document forensics, capacity plausibility, and regulatory history — before you buy.

tyre.title: Tyre / TPO EPR
tyre.body: The same independent risk-check, applied to tyre recyclers and their claimed Tyre Pyrolysis Oil output.

usedOil.title: Used-Oil EPR
usedOil.body: A guided assistant for CPCB's CA-1/CA-2 collection-agent registration — most applications stall in paperwork, not rejection. We help you get it right the first time.

howItWorks.heading: How it works
howItWorks.subheading: Three checks, one report — for battery and tyre recyclers. Designed to be completed before a certificate purchase, not after.

submit.title: Submit recycler name or certificate ID
submit.desc: Enter the recycler name, BWMR registration number, and batch details you want to verify. No portal access required.

checks.title: E-PRid runs three independent checks
checks.desc: Document forensics (EXIF, geotag, timestamps), capacity plausibility against public CPCB data, and regulatory history from enforcement databases.

score.title: Risk score and downloadable report
score.desc: Get a Low / Medium / High risk rating with a per-check breakdown and a PDF report you can attach to your compliance file.

whoItsFor.heading: Who it's for
whoItsFor.subheading: Every participant in the EPR certificate chain has something at stake.

consultancies.title: EPR Compliance Consultancies
consultancies.body: White-label the check. Run it for every client before filing. One bad certificate can cost your client an Environmental Compensation notice — and your firm its reputation.

producers.title: Producers & EV OEMs
producers.body: Your EPR obligation doesn't end at buying a certificate — for battery or tyre. If the recycler behind it is fraudulent, the liability lands on you. Verify before you file.

recyclers.title: Recyclers
recyclers.body: Stand out from the crowd. Get a Verified Recycler badge that producers can trust — because you've passed an independent check, not just self-reported.

getStarted.heading: Ready to check a recycler?
getStarted.body: Run a risk check on any recycler or certificate before you buy. Sign up and get your first report in minutes.

footer.title: E-PRid — EPR Certificate Verification
footer.subtitle: Covering Battery, Tyre & Used-Oil EPR compliance in India

vault.signInTitle: Sign in to access your vault
vault.signInBody: The document vault is a private space for recyclers to store registration, GST, and processing receipts.
vault.title: Document Vault
vault.subtitle: Store registration, GST certificates and processing receipts in one place.
vault.whichRecycler: Which recycler is this vault for?
vault.enterRecyclerId: Enter the recycler ID from a previous verification check.
vault.acceptDisclosurePrompt: Accept the disclosure to start uploading documents.
vault.setRecyclerIdPrompt: Set a recycler ID above to upload documents.

consent.title: Document vault — disclosure
consent.p1: Documents you upload are stored for your own filing convenience. No document is shared with any third party today.
consent.p2: In a future product update, you may be able to use stored documents as verification evidence in an E-PRid risk check. If this capability is added, you will be informed before any document is used in that way.
consent.p3: By uploading, you confirm you have the right to store these documents and you accept the above terms. This acceptance is logged with a timestamp as part of the audit trail.
consent.checkboxLabel: I understand and accept the above disclosure.

upload.fileHint: JPEG, PNG, WebP, TIFF, PDF · max 20 MB
upload.notesPlaceholder: Any reference notes for your own use

docList.confirmDelete: Remove this document from the vault?

verify.signInTitle: Sign in to verify a recycler
verify.signInBody: Verification checks are logged against your account so you have a complete due-diligence audit trail.
verify.title: Verify a Recycler
verify.subtitle: Evidence-backed risk check — each step is logged for your due-diligence file.

cpcbDirectory.signInTitle: Sign in to search the recycler directory
cpcbDirectory.signInBody: Search CPCB's public battery-recycler registry for registration/authorization status before you rely on a certificate.
cpcbDirectory.title: CPCB Recycler Directory
cpcbDirectory.subtitle: Entity Risk Score — registration, authorization, and geographic risk. Not the full Certificate Risk Score (that needs certificate-volume and invoice data this registry doesn't have).
cpcbDirectory.partialCaptureNotice: Several fields on this record weren't captured together in the same data pass — blanks below are unconfirmed, not verified-absent.
cpcbDirectory.scoreDirectionNote: Higher the score, higher the risk.
cpcbDirectory.noIssues: No flags — all available signals check out.
cpcbDirectory.scoreConfidenceNote: Entity Risk Score only — does not include certificate-volume or invoice-based risk.

recyclerDetailsForm.plausibilityIntro: We ran an immediate plausibility check on the batch figures you entered. Review the results below before uploading evidence.
recyclerDetailsForm.cardSubtitle: Enter the recycler's details and the batch they're claiming.

fields.tyreImported: The underlying waste tyre was imported

evidenceUpload.cardSubtitle: Add up to {max} files. Label each one so we apply the right date checks — a registration certificate from 2022 shouldn't be flagged just because it predates this batch.
evidenceUpload.dropzoneCta: Drag & drop files, or <link>click to browse</link>
evidenceUpload.runForensics: Run forensics checks ({count} {count, plural, one {file} other {files}})

compositeScore.heading: Composite risk score
compositeScore.notYetComputed: Composite score not yet available for this check.

evidenceSummary.empty: No evidence uploaded for this check.

status.PASS: All batch details are within plausible industry ranges.
status.WARN: Some batch details are unusual — review the findings below.
status.FAIL: One or more batch details appear implausible — this check has failed.
status.UNVERIFIABLE: Some checks could not be verified — additional data is needed.

regulatoryHistory.heading: Regulatory history
regulatoryHistory.notStarted: No regulatory research has been run yet. Click 'Run check' to start.
regulatoryHistory.pending: Researching CPCB enforcement records, NGT orders, and news…
regulatoryHistory.failedStatus: Research could not be completed — API key may not be configured.
regulatoryHistory.riskMedium: Medium regulatory risk — some findings of note.
regulatoryHistory.riskLow: Low regulatory risk — no significant enforcement history found.
regulatoryHistory.startFailed: Failed to start regulatory research. Please try again.

gpsLocation.what: Reads the GPS coordinates hidden inside the image (EXIF data) and checks whether the photo was taken in the same Indian state where this recycler is registered.
gpsLocation.why: A recycler registered in Tamil Nadu uploading a photo taken in Delhi is a red flag. If GPS data is missing (common when photos are shared via WhatsApp), the check reports 'unverifiable' — it does not silently pass.

timestampCheck.what: Reads the date and time the photo was taken from hidden EXIF metadata, then checks it against the claimed processing date using a tolerance window that depends on document type — site photos must be within ±7 days, invoices within ±30 days, registration certificates just need to predate the batch.
timestampCheck.why: A weighbridge slip created 41 days after the processing date, or a site photo taken a month before the batch existed, suggests the document was not produced at the time of processing.

pdfCreationDate.what: Reads the creation date stored in the PDF's metadata and checks it against the claimed processing date using the tolerance window for this document type.
pdfCreationDate.why: A legitimate weighbridge slip PDF would have been generated on or very close to the processing date. A PDF created weeks later suggests it was typed up retrospectively, even if it claims to document an earlier event.

pdfModificationConsistency.what: Checks whether the PDF's last-modified date is the same as or after its creation date.
pdfModificationConsistency.why: If a PDF shows a modification date that is earlier than its creation date, that is technically impossible under normal circumstances and strongly indicates the metadata has been manually altered — a common method used to backdate documents.

pdfAuthorship.what: Reads the author name and software used to create the PDF from its metadata.
pdfAuthorship.why: Legitimate documents from registered recyclers or certified weighbridge systems usually carry the name of the organisation or software that produced them. An author listed as 'anonymous' or missing entirely does not automatically mean fraud, but it removes one layer of traceability.

deviceIdentification.what: Reads the camera make and model from the image's EXIF metadata.
deviceIdentification.why: This is informational — it tells you what device took the photo. If multiple photos across different batches consistently come from the same personal phone rather than facility CCTV or an official device, that pattern is worth noting.

reverseImageDuplicate.what: Computes a visual fingerprint of the image (a perceptual hash) and compares it against every other image already uploaded across all checks in this system.
reverseImageDuplicate.why: In the plastic EPR fraud cases, recyclers reused the same facility photos across hundreds of certificate claims to make it appear that multiple processing events occurred. If this image is visually identical to one already on file for a different check or recycler, it is a significant red flag.

exifExtraction.what: The system attempted to read the hidden metadata embedded in this image file.
exifExtraction.why: If extraction fails entirely, it usually means the file is corrupt, in an unexpected format, or the metadata was deliberately stripped. All downstream checks (GPS, timestamp, device) will be unverifiable as a result.
